---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(pracma)
library(signal) 
```

```{r}
theme_black = function(base_size = 12, base_family = "") {
 
  theme_grey(base_size = base_size, base_family = base_family) %+replace%
 
    theme(
      # Specify axis options
      axis.line = element_blank(),  
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, color = "white", lineheight = 0.9),  
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, color = "white", lineheight = 0.9),  
      axis.ticks = element_line(color = "white", size  =  0.2),  
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = base_size, color = "white", margin = margin(0, 10, 0, 0)),  
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = base_size, color = "white", angle = 90, margin = margin(0, 10, 0, 0)),  
      axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "lines"),   
      # Specify legend options
      legend.background = element_rect(color = NA, fill = "black"),  
      legend.key = element_rect(color = "black",  fill = "black"),  
      legend.key.size = unit(1.2, "lines"),  
      legend.key.height = NULL,  
      legend.key.width = NULL,      
      legend.text = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, color = "white"),  
      legend.title = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, face = "bold", hjust = 0, color = "white"),  
      legend.position = "right",  
      legend.text.align = NULL,  
      legend.title.align = NULL,  
      legend.direction = "vertical",  
      legend.box = NULL, 
      # Specify panel options
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "black", color  =  NA),  
      panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "white"),  
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),  
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),  
      panel.margin = unit(0.5, "lines"),   
      # Specify facetting options
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey30", color = "grey10"),  
      strip.text.x = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, color = "white"),  
      strip.text.y = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, color = "white",angle = -90),  
      # Specify plot options
      plot.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "black"),  
      plot.title = element_text(size = base_size*1.2, color = "white"),  
      plot.margin = unit(rep(1, 4), "lines")
    )
}
```

# Read in Data
```{r}
setwd("/Users/elynorein/Desktop/R_ModelVideoPts")
xypts <- read_csv("Set2_H_Freq4_xypts.csv")
xypts <- na.omit(xypts)
colnames(xypts) <- c("X1", "Y1", "X2", "Y2")

# Use Frame rate to calculate a time vector
fps <- 30  
xypts$time <- (0:(nrow(xypts)-1)) / fps

# Spatial distance between head and tail points (length of the model, used later)
distance <- 0.235  # replace with actual length of the models
```

```{r}
# Smooth any weird bumps
xypts$Y1_smooth <- sgolayfilt(xypts$Y1, p = 3, n = 11)  # polynomial 3, window 11
xypts$Y2_smooth <- sgolayfilt(xypts$Y2, p = 3, n = 11)

# Detrend Y1
fit1 <- lm(Y1_smooth ~ time, data = xypts)   # Fit a linear trend
xypts$Y1_det <- xypts$Y1_smooth - predict(fit1)  # Subtract the trend

# Detrend Y2
fit2 <- lm(Y2_smooth ~ time, data = xypts)
xypts$Y2_det <- xypts$Y2_smooth - predict(fit2)

```

Plot to see if things look ok
```{r}
ggplot(xypts, aes(x = time)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = Y1_det), color = "blue", shape = 16) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Y1_det), color = "blue") +
  geom_point(aes(y = Y2_det), color = "red", shape = 17) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Y2_det), color = "red") +
  labs(x = "Time (s)", y = "Detrended Y") +
  theme_black()

ggplot(xypts, aes(x = time)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Y1_det), color = "blue") +
  geom_point(aes(y = Y1_det), color = "blue", shape = 16) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Y2_det), color = "red") +
  geom_point(aes(y = Y2_det), color = "red", shape = 17) +
  labs(x = "Time (s)", y = "Detrended Y") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1, 20)) +
  theme_black()

```

Y1 Amplitude

```{r}
# Find peaks for Y1
peaks_Y1 <- findpeaks(xypts$Y1_det, minpeakdistance = 5) # minpeakdistance in frames

# Find troughs for Y1 (invert the signal)
troughs_Y1 <- findpeaks(-xypts$Y1_det, minpeakdistance = 5)
troughs_Y1[,1] <- -troughs_Y1[,1]  # revert to original values

# Calculate amplitudes for each cycle (peak - closest trough)

amplitudes_Y1 <- rep(NA, nrow(peaks_Y1))  

for(i in 1:nrow(peaks_Y1)){
  peak_idx <- peaks_Y1[i, 2]
  valid <- troughs_Y1[troughs_Y1[,2] < peak_idx, , drop = FALSE] # troughs BEFORE this peak
  if(nrow(valid) == 0) next # if none exist, skip (leave NA)
  prev_trough_idx <- valid[which.max(valid[,2]), 2] # closest previous trough = one with max index
  peak_val   <- peaks_Y1[i, 1]
  trough_val <- troughs_Y1[troughs_Y1[,2] == prev_trough_idx, 1]
  amplitudes_Y1[i] <- (peak_val - trough_val) / 2
}

# Average amplitude
avg_amp_Y1 <- mean(amplitudes_Y1, na.rm = TRUE)

# Time of each peak (in seconds)
peaks_Y1 <- peaks_Y1[order(peaks_Y1[, 2]), ]
peak_times_Y1 <- xypts$time[peaks_Y1[,2]]

# Period = average time between peaks
period_Y1 <- mean(diff(peak_times_Y1), na.rm = TRUE)

# Frequency = 1 / period
freq_Y1 <- 1 / period_Y1

```

Plot to make sure things look ok
```{r}
# Create small data frames for peaks and troughs
peaks_df <- data.frame(time = xypts$time[peaks_Y1[,2]], Y = peaks_Y1[,1])
troughs_df <- data.frame(time = xypts$time[troughs_Y1[,2]], Y = troughs_Y1[,1])

# Plot
ggplot(xypts, aes(x = time, y = Y1_det)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  geom_point(data = peaks_df, aes(x = time, y = Y), color = "red", size = 2) +
  geom_point(data = troughs_df, aes(x = time, y = Y), color = "green", size = 2) +
  labs(x = "Time (s)", y = "Detrended Y1", 
       title = "Y1 with Detected Peaks (red) and Troughs (green)") +
  theme_black()

```


```{r}
# Find peaks for Y2
peaks_Y2 <- findpeaks(xypts$Y2_det, minpeakdistance = 5) # minpeakdistance in frames
# peaks_Y1[,1] = peak heights, peaks_Y1[,2] = indices

# Find troughs for Y1 (invert the signal)
troughs_Y2 <- findpeaks(-xypts$Y2_det, minpeakdistance = 5)
troughs_Y2[,1] <- -troughs_Y2[,1]  # revert to original values

# Calculate amplitudes for each cycle (peak - closest trough)
# Simplest approach: match peaks with closest previous trough
amplitudes_Y2 <- rep(NA, nrow(peaks_Y2))  
for(i in 1:nrow(peaks_Y2)){
  peak_idx <- peaks_Y2[i, 2]
  valid <- troughs_Y2[troughs_Y2[,2] < peak_idx, , drop = FALSE] # troughs BEFORE this peak
  if(nrow(valid) == 0) next # if none exist, skip (leave NA)
  prev_trough_idx <- valid[which.max(valid[,2]), 2] # closest previous trough = one with max index
  peak_val   <- peaks_Y2[i, 1]
  trough_val <- troughs_Y2[troughs_Y2[,2] == prev_trough_idx, 1]
  amplitudes_Y2[i] <- (peak_val - trough_val) / 2
}

# Average amplitude
avg_amp_Y2 <- mean(amplitudes_Y2, na.rm = TRUE)

# Time of each peak (in seconds)
peaks_Y2 <- peaks_Y2[order(peaks_Y2[, 2]), ]
peak_times_Y2 <- xypts$time[peaks_Y2[,2]]

# Period = average time between peaks
period_Y2 <- mean(diff(peak_times_Y2), na.rm = TRUE)

# Frequency = 1 / period
freq_Y2 <- 1 / period_Y2
```

Wavespeed Between Y1 and Y2
```{r}

# Cross-correlation between head (Y2) and tail (Y1)
ccf_result <- ccf(xypts$Y2_smooth, xypts$Y1_smooth, plot = FALSE)

# Find the lag with maximum correlation
lag_index <- which.max(ccf_result$acf)

# Time lag in seconds
time_lag <- ccf_result$lag[lag_index] / 30  # divide by frame rate

# Wave speed (m/s)
wave_speed <- distance / abs(time_lag)

```

Put into a table to copy paste more easily
```{r}

table_Y1 <- data.frame(
  Y1period = period_Y1,
  Y1freq   = freq_Y1,
  Y1amp = avg_amp_Y1,
  Y2period = period_Y2,
  Y2freq   = freq_Y2,
  Y2amp = avg_amp_Y2,
  waveSpeed = wave_speed
)

table_Y1

```
